narratives.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
narratives.evidence_quote: though L.A.’s infrastructure is not widely considered “high-quality.” A proposed high-speed rail between L.A. and San Francisco has been largely canceled, and current passenger rail service from L.A. to San Diego has been suspended because of a potential landslide hazard.
narratives.reasoning: This quote challenges the competence and credibility of Mayor Garcetti and his administration by highlighting failures in local infrastructure and transportation projects, undermining their authority on climate and urban policy.

narratives.evidence_quote: Garcetti’s “Green New Deal” calls for shuttering natural gas plants; it has been opposed by public works unions, and lost the only serious electoral challenge it faced in a 2019 city council race.
narratives.reasoning: The quote presents the climate policy (Green New Deal) as politically unpopular and opposed by key groups (unions), challenging the movement's support and practical political viability.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Criticism of climate policies: Climate policies are ineffective
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Garcetti joined a U.S. Department of Transportation presentation on 'high-quality urban transportation infrastructure,' though L.A.'s infrastructure is not widely considered 'high-quality.' A proposed high-speed rail between L.A. and San Francisco has been largely canceled, and current passenger rail service from L.A. to San Diego has been suspended because of a potential landslide hazard.
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly contrasts Garcetti's participation in a climate-focused infrastructure presentation with the poor state of actual infrastructure projects in his city, implying that the policies and actions being promoted are not delivering tangible, effective results.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Criticism of climate policies: Climate policies are only for profit
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Los Angeles Mayor Eric Garcetti joined a 'global coalition of mayors' at a summit on climate change this week in Buenos Aires, Argentina, burning fossil fuels on an international flight rather than attending remotely and attending to pressing issues at home.
subnarratives.reasoning: The text frames the trip as hypocritical and unnecessary, suggesting the summit is more about political posturing and international networking ('joining a global coalition') than genuine, effective climate action, which could be done remotely. This implies the activity is for the benefit of the participants' status or agenda rather than the environment.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Criticism of climate policies: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: According to the ICAO Carbon Emissions Calculator, Garcetti’s trip would have burned 1275.6 kg of carbon if he flew economy class, and twice as much if he flew business or 'premium' class (assuming a stop in Atlanta). That is one-fourth (economy) or one-half (premium) the global average per capita use of carbon per year. It is not clear why the conference could not be held remotely.
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote criticizes the climate policy actor (Garcetti) for personal hypocrisy and poor judgment in choosing a carbon-intensive travel method for a climate conference. It supports the parent narrative of criticizing climate policies/practices but does not fit the definitions of 'ineffective,' 'negative economic impact,' or 'only for profit.' It is a distinct argument about the hypocrisy of the actors implementing the policies.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities: Criticism of political organizations and figures
subnarratives.evidence_quote: White Garcetti was away, Los Angeles continued to struggle with the high crime and homelessness that have come to define his legacy, as well as a crisis in the City Council triggered by racist comments from City Council members within Garcetti’s party.
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly criticizes Mayor Eric Garcetti's performance and legacy, focusing on domestic failures (crime, homelessness, council crisis) while he is abroad, which discredits him as a political figure in the context of climate action.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: It is not clear why the conference could not be held remotely.
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement criticizes the decision-making and operational choices of the summit organizers (an institutional authority on climate action) by questioning the necessity of in-person travel, thereby expressing disapproval of their approach.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Criticism of climate movement: Climate movement is corrupt
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Los Angeles Mayor Eric Garcetti joined a “global coalition of mayors” at a summit on climate change this week in Buenos Aires, Argentina, burning fossil fuels on an international flight rather than attending remotely and attending to pressing issues at home.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Criticism of climate movement: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: White Garcetti was away, Los Angeles continued to struggle with the high crime and homelessness that have come to define his legacy, as well as a crisis in the City Council triggered by racist comments from City Council members within Garcetti’s party.
